name: Pointer Events API
performance_characteristics: Unified event stream with coalesced and predicted events for high-frequency input; efficient drag/draw handling via pointer capture
unsuitable_contexts: code paths that must run unchanged on very old browsers without a pointer-events polyfill
strengths: Baseline everywhere; one unified model for mouse, touch and pen input
Pointer capture simplifies drag, slider and gesture implementations
Exposes pressure, tilt, width/height and getCoalescedEvents for rich/high-rate input
Reduces the need for separate mouse and touch event handling
weaknesses: Gesture recognition (pinch, rotate, inertia) must still be built on top
Keyboard and assistive-tech interaction must be handled separately for accessibility
Cross-device quirks (hover-capable vs touch) still need feature detection
